title: baliospermum polyandrum
intro: Baliospermum polyandrum, also known as the 'Kerala spindle tree', is a plant species native to the Western Ghats of India. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with its leaves, roots, and seeds exhibiting anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and aiding in digestion, as well as exhibiting potential anti-cancer properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots are often used to make decoctions or infusions, and the seeds are used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various ailments.
